title: Collector lib: allowlist digest, realpath-contained, no free text
action: Create lib/feedback.mjs exporting SCHEMA_VERSION, ENTRY_FIELDS, DROP_REASONS, KIND_ALIASES, resolveInScope(root, relPath), listInScope(root, relDir), collectFeedback(root), buildDigest(root, {now}). CONTRACT OF THE GUARD (cell-review fix 1): resolveInScope VALIDATES AND RETURNS AN ABSOLUTE PATH — it never returns bytes. It may call only fs.realpath / fs.realpathSync / fs.lstatSync. All CONTENT reads go through the existing fsutil wrappers (readJson, readJsonl) fed a resolveInScope'd path; those wrappers hold the only readFileSync and they live in fsutil.mjs, not here. Directory enumeration (.bee/cells/*.json) must NOT be a bare readdir at the call site — it goes through the sibling exported guard listInScope(root, relDir), which resolves the dir in scope and returns entry names. The source-level assertion then greps feedback.mjs for /\bfs\s*\.\s*(readFile|readFileSync|readdir|readdirSync|createReadStream|openSync|readSync)\b/ and asserts ZERO matches — realpath/realpathSync/lstatSync are absent from that method list, so the guard's own calls never trip it and no per-function exclusion is needed. Also reject named imports of those methods from 'node:fs' (the alias hole). Like the COMMAND_KEYS drift guard this is a no-accidental-drift check, not a sandbox; say so in a comment. ABSENT SOURCES (cell-review fix 2, a real contradiction if unhandled): fs.realpath THROWS ENOENT on a path that does not exist, and .bee/decisions.jsonl or docs/history/learnings/ legitimately may not exist. A non-existent source is ABSENT — skipped and counted, never a scope violation and never a throw. Catch ENOENT specifically and skip; every other realpath error (EACCES, and any containment failure) still rejects. Without this rule the 'empty repo -> no throw' truth is unsatisfiable. Per decision 8cd4c84e the digest is an ALLOWLIST: each entry is exactly the exported ENTRY_FIELDS = ['kind','layer','source','title','first_seen','pain'] (cell-review fix 3 — evolving-3 imports this constant rather than hardcoding a second copy that would silently drift; pin the literal with a drift test the way BACKLOG_STATUSES is pinned). THERE IS NO detail/text/outcome/deviations FIELD — never collect free prose. Validating measured that friction detail is unfenced prose naming readBacklogCounts, COMMAND_KEYS, approved_gates.shape and internal call graphs, which no code-block strip and no secret regex removes; the surface is removed rather than filtered. READ SCOPE: every filesystem read routes through the single exported resolveInScope(root, relPath), which path.resolve()s the target, fs.realpath()s it AND the repo root, and throws unless the real target sits under realpath(root)/.bee/ or realpath(root)/docs/history/. path.resolve normalizes .. but does NOT resolve symlinks — a symlinked .bee/cells/evil.json pointing outside the repo must be rejected. No bare fs.<read> call may appear in this module outside resolveInScope. SOURCES: .bee/backlog.jsonl, .bee/decisions.jsonl, .bee/cells/*.json (trace.blocked_reason presence and trace.deviations LENGTH only — never their text), docs/history/learnings/*.md frontmatter. residual-findings.md is referenced by path as source, never opened for content. KIND: normalize entry.type through KIND_ALIASES (review-finding->finding, debt->debt, harness-issue->harness-issue, ...) into a closed enum; an unrecognized type is NOT silently dropped — it goes to dropped[] with reason 'unknown_type'. anphabe-gogl already carries 11 types and does not use 'finding' at all. LAYER: entry.layer or null (optional by contract). FIRST_SEEN: ts (backlog) | date (learnings) | capped_at ?? claimed_at (cells). PAIN: finding.severity P1->3, P2->2, P3->1; learnings frontmatter severity {low:1, medium:2, high:3}; DEFAULT 1 for friction and everything else. Do NOT compute frequency, corroboration, rank, clusters or tie-breaks — those moved to slice B. SAFETY on title, in this order: (1) scan with SECRET_CONTENT_PATTERNS and INJECTION_PATTERNS imported verbatim from lib/decisions.mjs (never redefine) BEFORE any transformation, so a match is counted as a security event rather than silently rewritten; (2) cap at 200 chars, marking truncation; (3) an unsafe entry is dropped. dropped is an ARRAY of {kind, layer, source, first_seen, reason} with reason in DROP_REASONS = ['secret','injection','oversize','unknown_type'] — the category only, NEVER the matched text; a bare integer cannot distinguish a careless worker from a repo probing bee every close. DIGEST: {schema_version, generated_at, repo_label, counts, dropped, entries}. buildDigest takes an injected clock {now} so the test can pin it; it is a SNAPSHOT, rebuilt from scratch each call, never appended. Malformed JSONL lines and cells without a trace are skipped and counted, never thrown. Extend tests/test_lib.mjs with every test-matrix row assigned to this cell: empty repo; malformed backlog line; trace-less cell; a friction row whose detail names readBacklogCounts and COMMAND_KEYS -> no detail field exists in the digest and neither string appears in JSON.stringify(digest); API key in a title -> dropped[0].reason==='secret', key absent from bytes; secret scan runs before transformation; trace.worker never appears in the digest bytes; resolveInScope throws on '../', on an absolute path, and on a sibling dir; a symlinked .bee/cells/evil.json pointing outside the repo is rejected and warned, not read; feedback.mjs source contains no bare fs.<read> outside resolveInScope (source-level assertion, mirroring the COMMAND_KEYS drift guard at test_onboard_bee.mjs:135-140); review-finding maps to finding; an invented type lands in dropped with reason 'unknown_type' and is counted; title over 200 chars truncated and marked; buildDigest(root,{now}) twice with a pinned clock -> byte-identical; pain mapping for all three scales; first_seen mapping per kind; dropped[] shape and DROP_REASONS pinned by a drift test; SCHEMA_VERSION pinned like BACKLOG_STATUSES.

truths: The digest contains no detail/text/outcome/deviations field; a friction row naming readBacklogCounts and COMMAND_KEYS leaves neither string in the digest bytes
Every read routes through resolveInScope, and a source-level test asserts no bare fs.<read> call exists outside it
A symlink under .bee/ resolving outside the repo is rejected by realpath containment, warned, and never read
The secret and injection scan runs before any transformation, so a match is counted as a security event
dropped is an array of {kind, layer, source, first_seen, reason} carrying the category only, never matched text
An unrecognized entry type is counted in dropped with reason 'unknown_type', never silently discarded
buildDigest(root,{now}) twice with a pinned clock yields byte-identical output; generated_at is the only volatile field
No frequency, corroboration, rank, cluster, or tie-break logic exists in this cell
buildDigest does not throw on a repo whose .bee/decisions.jsonl or docs/history/learnings/ does not exist — absent sources are skipped and counted
ENTRY_FIELDS is exported and pinned by a drift test; resolveInScope returns a path, never bytes

artifacts.substantive: SCHEMA_VERSION, DROP_REASONS, KIND_ALIASES, resolveInScope with realpath containment, collectFeedback, buildDigest with injected clock; patterns imported from decisions.mjs
assertions for allowlist, scope, symlink, scan ordering, dropped shape, kind aliasing, pain and first_seen mapping, idempotence, worker-PII, schema drift

key_links: feedback.mjs imports SECRET_CONTENT_PATTERNS and INJECTION_PATTERNS from lib/decisions.mjs — the same single source capture.mjs uses
resolveInScope is the only filesystem chokepoint, which is what makes the read-scope invariant provable at all
the source-level no-bare-fs assertion follows the COMMAND_KEYS drift-guard precedent in test_onboard_bee.mjs
prohibitions: Never collect a free-text field — no detail, text, outcome, or deviations prose
Never read a path whose realpath escapes .bee/ or docs/history/
Never redefine the secret or injection regexes locally
Never record matched secret text in dropped — the reason category only
Never compute ranking terms other than per-entry pain
Never append to a prior digest — buildDigest is a snapshot

trace.outcome: lib/feedback.mjs collector: resolveInScope realpath-containment chokepoint (+listInScope) so every read is scope-proven; buildDigest snapshot with injected clock is byte-identical; allowlist entries = ENTRY_FIELDS only, NO free-text field (D2/8cd4c84e); title scanned for secrets/injection BEFORE cap, unsafe dropped with category-only reason; KIND_ALIASES normalize types, unknown->unknown_type counted; pain P/lmh/default, first_seen per kind; trace.worker never emitted. 15 tests added.
deviations: Added readText(file, fallback='') to skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/fsutil.mjs — a THIRD file beyond the cell's declared two (feedback.mjs, test_lib.mjs). Rationale (deviation rule 2: missing critical functionality the outcome depends on): learnings *.md frontmatter is a mandated source, but fsutil exposed only JSON readers (readJson/readJsonl) and the read-scope drift guard forbids any bare content read inside feedback.mjs. The cell's own contract states content-read wrappers 'live in fsutil.mjs, not here', so the sanctioned fix is a minimal additive text reader there — rather than gaming the security grep (e.g. fs.promises.readFile) or blocking a high-risk cell on a 5-line helper. Purely additive; reserved before writing; no existing test changed; fsutil.mjs still 'copied verbatim' in the onboard suite.

Interpretation recorded for review: .bee/decisions.jsonl is listed in the cell SOURCES and in the absent-source must-have, but emits NO digest entries in slice A. Decision decision/rationale prose names the exact identifiers (readBacklogCounts, COMMAND_KEYS, approved_gates.shape) the D2 allowlist removed, and no allowlist field maps a decision event; ingesting it as a title would reintroduce the identifier leak and break the 'neither string appears in the digest bytes' invariant. It is still routed through resolveInScope so the absent-source skip-and-count path is genuinely exercised.
trace.friction: Real friction: the cell restricts content reads to fsutil's readJson/readJsonl and forbids bare fs reads in feedback.mjs, but a mandated source (learnings *.md frontmatter) is not JSON — fsutil had no text reader and I was scoped to 2 files. Resolving it honestly (add fsutil.readText vs game the grep vs block) cost real deliberation. The cell/plan assumed JSON-only sources; a one-line note that markdown needs a fsutil text primitive would have removed the ambiguity. Also: decisions.jsonl is listed as a source but has no allowlist field mapping and would leak identifiers if ingested — its role needed interpretation.
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 85 passed, 0 failed (15 new feedback tests). test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0; '.bee/bin/lib/feedback.mjs copied verbatim' and 'fsutil.mjs copied verbatim' both ok.
